metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A goblins city where Goblin Tinker-Vault of Seven Bright Locks, Black Market Arcade Beacon, Gear Inlays Command Bastion, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Klinkhollow should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Klinkhollow organizes power through Gear Inlays Command Bastion, public response through Black Market Arcade Beacon, civic memory through Goblin Tinker-Vault of Seven Bright Locks, and daily life through S1 Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, S2 Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, S3 Security / Intelligence Hall, S4 Low Residential Civic Hall, S5 Prison / Containment Civic Hall.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Iron, Red Brick, Dark Wood, Painted Plaster, Arcane Glow, Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Mechanical Animus, and exact anchors including Gear Inlays Command Bastion, Black Market Arcade Beacon, Goblin Tinker-Vault of Seven Bright Locks, Containment Keep, Scrap Market, Tenement Court, Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook, Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A goblins civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Goblin Tinker-Vault of Seven Bright Locks, Black Market Arcade Beacon, Gear Inlays Command Bastion, and section anchors such as Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Security / Intelligence Hall, Low Residential Civic Hall, Prison / Containment Civic Hall.

canonLocks: Klinkhollow uses city code KLI.
The Wonder is Goblin Tinker-Vault of Seven Bright Locks.
The Beacon is Black Market Arcade Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Gear Inlays Command Bastion and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.

The source-backed section spine is: S1 Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, S2 Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, S3 Security / Intelligence Hall, S4 Low Residential Civic Hall, S5 Prison / Containment Civic Hall.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Klinkhollow as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

environment.environmentalLogic: The environment should make Klinkhollow's exact civic anchors plausible at street scale: terrain routes people toward section landmarks, lighting clarifies public authority, and water or landscape features remain source-backed rather than decorative filler.

skyline: Goblin Tinker-Vault of Seven Bright Locks

colorLanguage: local textile, stone, metal, vegetation, or water colors from the image package

civilization.people: The population should read as goblins and city-specific: citizens occupy streets, shops, public thresholds, and landmark approaches as people who understand the local civic order.
socialClasses: laborers, carriers, messengers, and maintenance crews
households tied to local district obligations
civilization.governance: Government is centered on Gear Inlays Command Bastion. Its authority should be visible through courts, records, public access routes, civic lamps, guards or attendants, and the way citizens respond to D13.
civilization.religion: Religious or ritual life should only be emphasized where source anchors support it, especially names such as none explicitly dominant in the current anchors.

streets.appearance: Streets should show Klinkhollow's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, S2 Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, S3 Security / Intelligence Hall, S4 Low Residential Civic Hall, S5 Prison / Containment Civic Hall. Keep roads, plazas, stairs, thresholds, lamps, signs-without-readable-text, props, and civilians aligned to the source-backed materials and landmarks.
streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.
sounds: landmark-specific sounds from water, bells, animals, machines, or crowds when source-backed
smells: worn stone, timber, metal, plaster, or fabric
streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce the local terrain and materials without overwhelming the city identity or inventing unsupported climate extremes.
streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

common: street services tied to section anchors
exports: specialized tools or ritual goods as supported by anchors
imports: dyes, oils, spices, timber, stone, metal, glass, or cloth as needed by the architecture

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Gear Inlays Command Bastion, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.
